items.title: Shuttle
items.text:
Secure SSH gateway for AI assistants

Shuttle lets AI assistants (Claude Code, Cursor, etc.) securely execute commands on your remote SSH servers --- with connection pooling, session isolation, command safety rules, and a web audit panel.

items.title: Features
items.text:
• 4-Level Command Security --- Block dangerous commands, require confirmation for risky ones, warn on installs, allow the rest
  • Connection Pooling --- Reuse SSH connections across commands, no repeated handshakes
  • Session Isolation --- Each AI conversation gets its own working directory context
  • Web Audit Panel --- See every command the AI ran, per node, with full stdout/stderr
  • Per-Node Rules --- Different security policies for prod vs dev servers
  • Jump Host Support --- Connect through bastion/jump servers

items.title: 1. Install
items.text:
# Via uvx (recommended)
uvx shuttle-mcp

# Or pip
pip install shuttle-mcp

items.title: 2. Add your first node
items.text:
shuttle node add
# Follow the prompts: name, host, username, password/key

items.title: 3. Connect to your AI assistant
items.text:
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "shuttle": {
      "command": "uvx",
      "args": ["shuttle-mcp"]
    }
  }
}

See MCP Setup for detailed instructions for Claude Code, Cursor, and both transport modes.

items.title: MCP Tools
items.text:
AI assistants get these tools automatically:

Tool Description ssh_execute Run a command on a remote node ssh_upload Upload a file via SFTP ssh_download Download a file via SFTP ssh_list_nodes List all configured nodes ssh_add_node Add a new SSH node ssh_remove_node Remove a node ssh_session_start Start a stateful session (preserves working directory) ssh_session_end End a session ssh_session_list List active sessions

items.title: Architecture
items.text:
Developer <-> AI Assistant <-> Shuttle (MCP) <-> SSH <-> Remote Servers
                                  |
                        +--------------------+
                        |   Core Engine      |
                        |  - ConnectionPool  |
                        |  - SessionManager  |
                        |  - CommandGuard    |
                        |  - SQLAlchemy ORM  |
                        +--------------------+

Service mode runs a single ASGI app serving both MCP (at /mcp/) and the Web UI (at /) on the same port.

items.title: Documentation
items.text:
• Configuration --- Environment variables, database URLs, pool parameters
  • Security Rules --- 4-level rule system, regex patterns, per-node overrides
  • MCP Setup --- Claude Code, Cursor, stdio and HTTP modes
  • Web Panel --- Overview, activity logs, rule management, settings

def register_execute_command_tool(mcp: FastMCP, ssh_manager: SSHConnectionManager):
    @mcp.tool("execute-command")
    async def execute_command(...):
        pass

# 需要手动调用
register_execute_command_tool(self.mcp, self._ssh_manager)

items.title: v2 最佳实践实现 ✅
items.text: 基于SSH MCP Tools v2的成功实现，以下是经过验证的最佳实践：
path: FastMCP SSH Tools 最佳实践指南

items.title: 1. 直接装饰器模式 ✅
items.text:
# 最佳实践：直接在模块级别定义工具
@mcp.tool(
    name="execute-command",
    description="Execute command on remote SSH server and return raw output",
    # ... 其他参数
)
async def execute_command(...):
    pass
path: FastMCP SSH Tools 最佳实践指南

items.title: 2. Context依赖注入 ✅
items.text:
# 最佳实践：使用Context获取依赖和会话信息
async def execute_command(
    cmdString: str,
    connectionName: Optional[str] = None,
    ctx: Context = None  # Context依赖注入
):
    # 使用Context进行日志记录
    if ctx:
        await ctx.info(f"Executing command: {cmdString}")
path: FastMCP SSH Tools 最佳实践指南

items.title: 3. 丰富的工具元数据 ✅
items.text:
@mcp.tool(
    name="execute-command",
    description="Execute command on remote SSH server and return raw output",
    annotations=ToolAnnotations(
        title="SSH Command Executor",
        readOnlyHint=False,
        destructiveHint=True,
        idempotentHint=False,
        openWorldHint=True
    ),
    tags={"ssh", "remote", "command"},
    meta={"version": "2.0", "category": "remote-execution"}
)
path: FastMCP SSH Tools 最佳实践指南

items.title: 4. 全局状态管理 ✅
items.text:
# 使用全局变量或Context状态管理依赖
_ssh_manager = None

def set_ssh_manager(ssh_manager):
    global _ssh_manager
    _ssh_manager = ssh_manager

def get_ssh_manager():
    return _ssh_manager
path: FastMCP SSH Tools 最佳实践指南

items.title: MCP传输协议完整对比指南
path: MCP传输协议完整对比指南

items.title: 🎯 协议概述
items.text: FastMCP支持三种主要传输协议，每种都有特定的使用场景和优势。
path: MCP传输协议完整对比指南

items.title: 📋 详细协议对比
path: MCP传输协议完整对比指南

items.title: 1. STDIO（推荐用于Cursor）
items.text:
特点： - 🔥 默认协议，最广泛兼容 - 🚀 极低延迟，本地通信 - 🔒 最安全，无网络暴露 - 🛠️ 简单调试，标准输入输出

工作原理： 

# Cursor启动命令
uv --directory /path/to/project run fastmcp-ssh-server --host ... --username ...

使用场景： - ✅ Cursor IDE集成（当前使用） - ✅ Claude Desktop集成 - ✅ 本地开发和测试 - ✅ 命令行工具集成

优势： - 无需网络端口 - 客户端管理服务器生命周期 - 极简配置 - 跨平台兼容

劣势： - 仅限本地使用 - 不支持远程访问 - 单一客户端连接
path: MCP传输协议完整对比指南

items.title: 2. Streamable HTTP（推荐用于Web部署）
items.text:
特点： - 🌐 现代Web协议 - 🔄 双向通信 - 📈 高可扩展性 - 🔗 网络访问

工作原理： 

# FastMCP服务器配置
mcp.run(transport="http", host="0.0.0.0", port=8000)
# 访问: http://localhost:8000/mcp

使用场景： - ✅ Web应用集成 - ✅ 微服务架构 - ✅ 远程MCP服务器 - ✅ 多客户端访问

优势： - 支持远程访问 - 多客户端并发 - 标准HTTP协议 - 良好的负载均衡支持

劣势： - 需要网络配置 - 更高的延迟 - 安全考虑更复杂
path: MCP传输协议完整对比指南

items.title: 3. SSE（已弃用，不推荐）
items.text:
特点： - ⚠️ 已弃用协议 - 📡 单向流式传输 - 🔄 实时事件推送

工作原理： 

# FastMCP服务器配置（不推荐）
mcp.run(transport="sse", host="127.0.0.1", port=8000)
# 访问: http://localhost:8000/sse

使用场景： - ❌ 新项目不推荐 - ❌ 已有SSE系统迁移过渡

为什么弃用： - 单向通信限制 - 浏览器兼容性问题 - Streamable HTTP更优秀
path: MCP传输协议完整对比指南

items.title: 🎯 针对你的情况的建议
path: MCP传输协议完整对比指南

items.title: 为什么STDIO最适合Cursor？
items.text:
1. 安全性：
  2. 无网络端口暴露
  3. 本地进程间通信

  4. 凭据不经过网络

  5. 性能：

  6. 极低延迟（< 1ms）
  7. 无网络开销

  8. 直接进程通信

  9. 简单性：

  10. 零网络配置
  11. 自动进程管理

  12. 无需防火墙设置

  13. 兼容性：

  14. 所有IDE原生支持
  15. 跨平台工作
  16. 标准MCP实现
path: MCP传输协议完整对比指南

items.title: 🔄 何时考虑其他协议？
path: MCP传输协议完整对比指南

items.title: 切换到HTTP的场景：
items.text:
# 如果需要远程访问
uv run fastmcp-ssh-server --transport http --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000 ...

# Cursor配置（通过mcp-remote代理）
{
  "remote-ssh-server": {
    "command": "npx",
    "args": ["-y", "mcp-remote", "http://your-server:8000/mcp"],
    "env": {"MCP_TRANSPORT_STRATEGY": "http-only"}
  }
}

使用HTTP的场景： - 团队共享MCP服务器 - 云端部署 - 跨网络访问 - 多客户端并发
path: MCP传输协议完整对比指南

items.title: 🛡️ 安全对比
items.text: 协议 安全等级 网络暴露 认证方式 推荐场景 STDIO 🔒 最高 无 进程级 本地开发 HTTP 🔐 中等 有 Token/OAuth 远程访问 SSE ⚠️ 弃用 有 Token/OAuth 不推荐
path: MCP传输协议完整对比指南

items.title: 📈 性能对比
items.text: 指标 STDIO HTTP SSE 延迟 < 1ms 5-50ms 10-100ms 吞吐量 极高 高 中等 并发 1 多个 多个 资源占用 最低 中等 较高
path: MCP传输协议完整对比指南

items.title: 🎉 结论
items.text:
你的选择是完美的！ STDIO协议是Cursor MCP集成的黄金标准：

  • ✅ 保持当前配置
  • ✅ 性能最优
  • ✅ 安全性最高
  • ✅ 配置最简单

除非有特殊需求（如远程访问、团队共享），否则不需要更改协议。
path: MCP传输协议完整对比指南

items.title: 🔧 快速诊断
items.text:
如果遇到问题，按优先级检查：

  1. STDIO问题： 

    # 测试服务器
uv run fastmcp-ssh-server --help

  2. HTTP备选方案： 

    # 启动HTTP服务器
uv run fastmcp-ssh-server --transport http --host 127.0.0.1 --port 8000 ...

  3. 调试连接： 

    # 使用FastMCP客户端测试
python -c "
import asyncio
from fastmcp import Client

async def test():
    client = Client('./your-server-script.py')
    async with client:
        await client.ping()
        print('MCP服务器正常工作！')

asyncio.run(test())
"
path: MCP传输协议完整对比指南

items.title: 🎯 Migration Overview

items.title: ✅ Migration Completed Successfully
items.text:
The SSH MCP Tools v2 implementation is now the unified standard for all users. The migration has been completed with the following achievements:

  • Enhanced Developer Experience: Modern decorator-based tool definition is now standard
  • Better Observability: Integrated structured logging with Context injection is active
  • Improved Maintainability: Self-contained tool definitions with metadata are in use
  • Advanced Features: Progress reporting and enhanced debugging capabilities are available
  • Future-Ready: Built on FastMCP best practices for extensibility
path: 🚀 SSH MCP Tools v2 Migration Guide

items.title: Migration Philosophy (Historical)
items.text:
The completed migration followed the principle of "Zero Disruption, Maximum Benefit":

✅ 100% API Compatibility - No changes were required to existing integrations ✅ Seamless Transition - v1 features were fully integrated into v2 implementation ✅ Enhanced Features - All capabilities preserved while gaining new functionality ✅ Unified Architecture - Single implementation provides all features
path: 🚀 SSH MCP Tools v2 Migration Guide

items.title: 🆕 What's New in v2
path: 🚀 SSH MCP Tools v2 Migration Guide

items.title: 1. Modern Tool Architecture
items.text:
v1 (Traditional): 

def register_execute_command_tool(mcp: FastMCP, ssh_manager: SSHConnectionManager):
    @mcp.tool("execute-command")
    async def execute_command(cmdString: str, connectionName: str | None = None):
        # Implementation

v2 (Enhanced): 

@mcp.tool("execute-command")
async def execute_command(
    cmdString: str,
    connectionName: str | None = None,
    ctx: Context | None = None,
) -> str:
    # Implementation with Context integration
path: 🚀 SSH MCP Tools v2 Migration Guide

items.title: 2. Context Dependency Injection
items.text: v2 tools receive a Context object providing: - Structured Logging: ctx.logger.info("Operation started") - Progress Reporting: ctx.progress.update(50, "Processing...") - State Management: Access to MCP server state and configuration - Enhanced Debugging: Rich error context and tracing
path: 🚀 SSH MCP Tools v2 Migration Guide

items.title: 3. Enhanced Tool Metadata
items.text:
@mcp.tool(
    "execute-command",
    description="Execute SSH commands with enhanced monitoring",
    annotations={
        "category": "ssh-operations",
        "security_level": "managed",
        "requires_auth": True
    },
    tags=["ssh", "command", "remote"],
    meta={
        "version": "2.0.0",
        "author": "FastMCP SSH Tools",
        "supports_progress": True
    }
)
path: 🚀 SSH MCP Tools v2 Migration Guide

items.title: 4. Automatic Tool Registration
items.text:
v1: Manual registration required 

# Multiple registration calls needed
register_execute_command_tool(mcp, ssh_manager)
register_upload_tool(mcp, ssh_manager)
register_download_tool(mcp, ssh_manager)
register_list_servers_tool(mcp, ssh_manager)

v2: Automatic via decorators 

# Tools auto-register when imported
from .ssh_tools import mcp  # All tools already registered

items.title: 📝 Migration Steps
path: 🚀 SSH MCP Tools v2 Migration Guide

items.title: Phase 1: Preparation (5 minutes)
items.text:
1. Backup Current Configuration 

    cp ~/.config/mcp/settings.json ~/.config/mcp/settings.json.backup

  2. Verify Current Setup 

    fastmcp-ssh-server --version
fastmcp-ssh-server --help | grep tools-version

  3. Test Current Functionality

  4. Verify all SSH connections work
  5. Test each tool: execute-command, upload, download, list-servers
  6. Document any custom configurations
path: 🚀 SSH MCP Tools v2 Migration Guide

items.title: Phase 2: Environment Testing (10 minutes)
items.text:
1. Test v2 in Development 

    # Test v2 with existing configuration
fastmcp-ssh-server --host YOUR_HOST --username YOUR_USER --tools-version v2

  2. Validate Tool Functionality 

    # Run the same tests as v1
# Verify identical outputs and behavior

  3. Monitor Performance

  4. Check startup time
  5. Observe memory usage
  6. Test command execution speed
path: 🚀 SSH MCP Tools v2 Migration Guide

items.title: Phase 3: Gradual Migration (Variable timing)
items.text: Choose one of these migration strategies:
path: 🚀 SSH MCP Tools v2 Migration Guide

items.title: Strategy A: Immediate Migration (Recommended for new deployments)
items.text:
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "ssh-mcp-server": {
      "command": "fastmcp-ssh-server",
      "args": [
        "--host", "your-server.com",
        "--username", "your-user",
        "--tools-version", "v2"
      ]
    }
  }
}
path: 🚀 SSH MCP Tools v2 Migration Guide

items.title: Strategy B: Environment-Based Migration (Recommended for production)
items.text:
1. Set up environment control: 

    export SSH_MCP_TOOLS_VERSION=v2

  2. Use auto mode: 

    {
  "mcpServers": {
    "ssh-mcp-server": {
      "command": "fastmcp-ssh-server",
      "args": [
        "--host", "your-server.com",
        "--username", "your-user",
        "--tools-version", "auto"
      ]
    }
  }
}

  3. Test and rollback if needed: 

    unset SSH_MCP_TOOLS_VERSION  # Falls back to v1
path: 🚀 SSH MCP Tools v2 Migration Guide

items.title: Strategy C: Blue-Green Deployment
items.text:
1. Deploy v2 alongside v1: 

    {
  "mcpServers": {
    "ssh-mcp-server-v1": {
      "command": "fastmcp-ssh-server",
      "args": ["--host", "server.com", "--username", "user", "--tools-version", "v1"]
    },
    "ssh-mcp-server-v2": {
      "command": "fastmcp-ssh-server",
      "args": ["--host", "server.com", "--username", "user", "--tools-version", "v2"]
    }
  }
}

  2. Test both versions

  3. Switch traffic to v2
  4. Remove v1 configuration
path: 🚀 SSH MCP Tools v2 Migration Guide

items.title: Phase 4: Validation (15 minutes)
items.text:
1. Functional Testing 

    # Test all operations
echo "ls -la" | fastmcp-ssh-server --host server --username user --tools-version v2

  2. Performance Validation

  3. Compare response times
  4. Monitor resource usage

  5. Check log output quality

  6. Integration Testing

  7. Test with your AI assistant
  8. Verify all workflows function correctly
  9. Check error handling

items.title: 🛠️ Common Issues and Solutions
path: 🚀 SSH MCP Tools v2 Migration Guide

items.title: Issue 1: "Context not available" warnings
items.text:
Symptoms: Warning messages about Context not being available

Solution: This is informational only in v2. Tools work without Context. 

# These warnings are safe to ignore:
# "Context not provided, using fallback logging"
path: 🚀 SSH MCP Tools v2 Migration Guide

items.title: Issue 2: Slightly increased memory usage
items.text:
Symptoms: Higher memory consumption with v2

Solution: Expected due to enhanced features. Monitor and optimize if needed: 

# Check memory usage
ps aux | grep fastmcp-ssh-server
path: 🚀 SSH MCP Tools v2 Migration Guide

items.title: Issue 3: Environment variable not taking effect
items.text:
Symptoms: --tools-version auto not using environment variable

Solution: Ensure correct variable name and value: 

export SSH_MCP_TOOLS_VERSION=v2  # Not SSH_TOOLS_VERSION
fastmcp-ssh-server --tools-version auto  # Must specify auto
path: 🚀 SSH MCP Tools v2 Migration Guide

items.title: Issue 4: Tool registration errors
items.text:
Symptoms: "Tool not found" errors with v2

Solution: Verify installation and imports: 

# Reinstall if needed
uv sync
uv run fastmcp-ssh-server --version
path: 🚀 SSH MCP Tools v2 Migration Guide

items.title: Issue 5: Performance regression
items.text:
Symptoms: Noticeably slower performance with v2

Solution: 1. Check system resources 2. Verify SSH connection stability 3. Consider reverting to v1 if performance is critical

# Quick performance test
time echo "date" | fastmcp-ssh-server --host server --username user --tools-version v2
path: 🚀 SSH MCP Tools v2 Migration Guide

items.title: 🎉 Conclusion
items.text:
Migrating to SSH MCP Tools v2 provides significant benefits with minimal risk:

  • Enhanced Development Experience with modern patterns
  • Better Observability through structured logging
  • Improved Maintainability with self-contained tool definitions
  • Future-Ready Architecture built on FastMCP best practices

The migration is designed to be safe, gradual, and reversible. Start with testing in a development environment, then proceed with confidence knowing you can rollback instantly if needed.

Next Steps: 1. Read docs/TOOLS_V2_FEATURES.md for detailed feature documentation 2. Test v2 in your environment 3. Plan your migration strategy 4. Execute the migration 5. Enjoy the enhanced capabilities!

items.title: 🚀 Key Innovations
path: 📊 SSH MCP Tools v2 Project Summary

items.title: 1. Seamless Version Coexistence
items.text:
The ability to run v1 and v2 side-by-side with instant switching: 

# Switch between versions instantly
fastmcp-ssh-server --host server --username user --tools-version v1
fastmcp-ssh-server --host server --username user --tools-version v2

# Environment-based control
SSH_MCP_TOOLS_VERSION=v2 fastmcp-ssh-server --tools-version auto
path: 📊 SSH MCP Tools v2 Project Summary

items.title: 2. Zero-Configuration v2 Tools
items.text:
Automatic tool registration eliminates boilerplate: 

# v1: Manual registration required
register_execute_command_tool(mcp, ssh_manager)
register_upload_tool(mcp, ssh_manager)
# ... 4 registration calls

# v2: Automatic registration
from .ssh_tools import mcp  # All tools registered!
path: 📊 SSH MCP Tools v2 Project Summary

items.title: 3. Context-Aware Operation
items.text:
Enhanced observability and debugging: 

# v1: Silent operation
result = await ssh_manager.execute_command(cmd)

# v2: Rich context and logging
if ctx:
    ctx.logger.info("Executing command", {"cmd": cmd})
    ctx.progress.update(50, "Processing...")
result = await ssh_manager.execute_command(cmd)
if ctx:
    ctx.logger.info("Command completed", {"output_size": len(result)})

items.title: 📦 PyPI Publishing Guide
items.text: Complete guide for publishing SSH MCP Tools to PyPI using GitHub Actions and trusted publishing.

items.title: 1. PyPI Account Setup
items.text:
1. Create accounts on both PyPI and TestPyPI
  2. Enable 2FA on both accounts
  3. Note your usernames for both platforms

items.title: 2. GitHub Repository Setup
items.text:
1. Ensure your repository is public (required for trusted publishing)
  2. Repository must be named exactly as specified in PyPI project settings
  3. Verify repository URL matches the one in pyproject.toml

items.title: For Production PyPI:
items.text:
1. Go to PyPI and log in
  2. Navigate to "Your projects" → "Manage" (or create new project)
  3. Go to "Settings" → "Publishing"
  4. Click "Add a new publisher"
  5. Fill in the details:
  6. Repository name: enwaiax/shuttle
  7. Workflow filename: publish-to-pypi.yml
  8. Environment name: pypi

items.title: For TestPyPI:
items.text:
1. Go to TestPyPI and log in
  2. Follow the same steps as above but use:
  3. Environment name: testpypi

items.title: 2. GitHub Environment Setup
items.text:
1. Go to your GitHub repository
  2. Navigate to "Settings" → "Environments"
  3. Create two environments:

items.title: Environment: pypi
items.text:
• Name: pypi
  • Protection rules:
  • ✅ Required reviewers (optional, for extra safety)
  • ✅ Wait timer (optional, for staged releases)

items.title: Environment: testpypi
items.text:
• Name: testpypi
  • Protection rules: None required for testing

items.title: Step 1: Prepare Release
items.text:
# Update version in pyproject.toml
sed -i 's/version = "[^"]*"/version = "1.0.0"/' pyproject.toml

# Update CHANGELOG.md
# Add release notes under ## [Unreleased] section

# Commit changes
git add pyproject.toml CHANGELOG.md
git commit -m "chore: prepare release v1.0.0"
git push origin main

items.title: Step 2: Create Release Tag
items.text:
# Create and push tag
git tag -a v1.0.0 -m "Release version 1.0.0"
git push origin v1.0.0

items.title: Step 3: Create GitHub Release
items.text:
1. Go to Releases
  2. Click "Create a new release"
  3. Select tag v1.0.0
  4. Add release title: SSH MCP Tools v1.0.0
  5. Add release notes from CHANGELOG.md
  6. Click "Publish release"

This triggers the automated workflow that will: 1. ✅ Run comprehensive tests 2. ✅ Build the package 3. ✅ Publish to TestPyPI 4. ✅ Publish to PyPI (production)

items.title: Option 2: Manual Workflow Trigger
items.text:
1. Go to "Actions" tab in your GitHub repository
  2. Select "Release" workflow
  3. Click "Run workflow"
  4. Enter version number (e.g., 1.0.0)
  5. Select release type (patch/minor/major)
  6. Click "Run workflow"

items.title: Option 3: Local Publishing (Not Recommended)
items.text:
# Install build tools
uv sync --dev

# Build package
uv build

# Check package
uv run twine check dist/*

# Upload to TestPyPI first
uv run twine upload --repository testpypi dist/*

# If successful, upload to PyPI
uv run twine upload dist/*

items.title: 1. Trusted Publishing Not Working
items.text:
Error: The user 'username' isn't allowed to upload to project 'fastmcp-ssh-server'
Solution: - Verify trusted publishing is configured correctly on PyPI - Check that repository name, workflow file, and environment name match exactly - Ensure the GitHub environment exists and has proper permissions

items.title: 2. Package Already Exists
items.text:
ERROR: File already exists
Solution: - You cannot overwrite existing versions on PyPI - Bump the version number in pyproject.toml - Create a new release

items.title: 3. Test Failures in CI
items.text:
Tests failed during GitHub Actions workflow
Solution: - Check the Actions tab for detailed error logs - Fix failing tests locally first - Push fixes and re-trigger the workflow

items.title: 4. Build Failures
items.text:
Package build failed
Solution: - Check pyproject.toml for syntax errors - Ensure all required files are included - Test build locally: uv build

# Validate package structure
uv run python -m tarfile -l dist/*.tar.gz

# Check package metadata
uv run python -c "import importlib.metadata; print(importlib.metadata.metadata('fastmcp-ssh-server'))"

# Test package installation
uv pip install dist/*.whl
uv run python -c "import python_ssh_mcp; print('Import successful')"

items.title: 🎯 Overview
items.text: The project uses automated GitHub Actions workflows to: 1. Run comprehensive tests across multiple platforms 2. Build the package 3. Publish to TestPyPI for validation 4. Publish to PyPI for production release

items.title: ✅ Prerequisites
items.text: Before releasing, ensure: - [ ] All tests pass locally: uv run pytest - [ ] Code quality checks pass: uv run ruff check and uv run ruff format --check - [ ] Type checking passes: uv run mypy src/ - [ ] Documentation is up to date - [ ] CHANGELOG.md is updated with new features/fixes - [ ] Version number is bumped in pyproject.toml

items.title: Semantic Versioning
items.text: This project follows Semantic Versioning: - MAJOR (1.0.0): Breaking changes - MINOR (0.1.0): New features, backward compatible - PATCH (0.0.1): Bug fixes, backward compatible

items.title: Release Channels
items.text:
1. Development: Direct pushes to main trigger tests
  2. Testing: TestPyPI for pre-release validation
  3. Production: PyPI for stable releases

items.title: 1. Prepare the Release
items.text:
# Ensure you're on the main branch and up to date
git checkout main
git pull origin main

# Update version in pyproject.toml
# Edit the version field: version = "x.y.z"

# Update CHANGELOG.md with release notes
# Document new features, bug fixes, and breaking changes

# Commit version bump
git add pyproject.toml CHANGELOG.md
git commit -m "chore: bump version to x.y.z"
git push origin main

items.title: 2. Create and Push Release Tag
items.text:
# Create a version tag
git tag -a v1.0.0 -m "Release version 1.0.0"

# Push the tag to GitHub
git push origin v1.0.0

items.title: 3. Create GitHub Release
items.text:
1. Go to GitHub Releases
  2. Click "Create a new release"
  3. Select the tag you just created
  4. Fill in release title: v1.0.0
  5. Add release notes from CHANGELOG.md
  6. Check "Set as the latest release" for stable releases
  7. Click "Publish release"

items.title: 🤖 Automated Release Workflow
items.text: When you create a GitHub release or push a version tag, the workflow automatically:

items.title: Phase 1: Testing
items.text:
• Runs tests on Ubuntu, Windows, and macOS
  • Performs code quality checks
  • Runs type checking
  • Generates coverage reports

items.title: Phase 2: Building
items.text:
• Builds the package using uv build
  • Creates both wheel (.whl) and source (.tar.gz) distributions

items.title: Phase 3: TestPyPI Publication
items.text:
• Publishes to TestPyPI for validation
  • URL: https://test.pypi.org/p/fastmcp-ssh-server

items.title: Phase 4: PyPI Publication
items.text:
• Publishes to production PyPI
  • URL: https://pypi.org/p/fastmcp-ssh-server

items.text:
If you need to release manually:

uv sync --dev

# Run tests
uv run pytest tests/ -v

# Build the package
uv build

# Check the built package
uv run twine check dist/*

# Upload to TestPyPI (optional)
uv run twine upload --repository testpypi dist/*

# Upload to PyPI
uv run twine upload dist/*

  • Core Architecture Improvements

path: 🌟 SSH MCP Tools v2 Features Guide

items.title: 🎯 Overview
items.text: SSH MCP Tools v2 represents a complete architectural evolution while maintaining 100% API compatibility with v1. Built on FastMCP best practices, v2 provides enhanced developer experience, better observability, and modern tooling capabilities.
path: 🌟 SSH MCP Tools v2 Features Guide

items.title: Key Principles
items.text:
• Zero Breaking Changes: 100% backward compatibility with v1 API
  • Enhanced Observability: Rich logging and monitoring capabilities
  • Modern Architecture: Decorator-based tool definitions
  • Developer Experience: Simplified development and debugging
  • Production Ready: Enterprise-grade reliability and performance
path: 🌟 SSH MCP Tools v2 Features Guide

items.title: 🏗️ Core Architecture Improvements
path: 🌟 SSH MCP Tools v2 Features Guide

items.title: Direct Decorator Pattern
items.text:
v1 Architecture (Function-based registration): 

def register_execute_command_tool(mcp: FastMCP, ssh_manager: SSHConnectionManager):
    @mcp.tool("execute-command")
    async def execute_command(cmdString: str, connectionName: str | None = None):
        try:
            result = await ssh_manager.execute_command(cmdString, connectionName)
            return result.strip() if result else ""
        except Exception as error:
            return f"Error: {str(error)}"
    return execute_command

v2 Architecture (Direct decorator pattern): 

@mcp.tool("execute-command")
async def execute_command(
    cmdString: str,
    connectionName: str | None = None,
    ctx: Context | None = None,
) -> str:
    """Execute SSH commands with enhanced monitoring and Context integration."""
    ssh_manager = get_ssh_manager()

    if ctx:
        ctx.logger.info("Executing SSH command", {"command": cmdString, "connection": connectionName})

    try:
        result = await ssh_manager.execute_command(cmdString, connectionName)

        if ctx:
            ctx.logger.info("Command execution completed", {"output_length": len(result)})

        return result.strip() if result else ""
    except Exception as error:
        if ctx:
            ctx.logger.error("Command execution failed", {"error": str(error)})
        return f"Error: {str(error)}"
path: 🌟 SSH MCP Tools v2 Features Guide

items.title: Benefits of Direct Decoration
items.text:
1. Self-Contained Tools: Each tool is a complete, standalone unit
  2. Automatic Registration: Tools register themselves when imported
  3. Cleaner Code: No boilerplate registration functions
  4. Better IDE Support: Enhanced autocomplete and type checking
  5. Easier Testing: Tools can be tested in isolation
path: 🌟 SSH MCP Tools v2 Features Guide

items.title: 🔌 Context Dependency Injection
path: 🌟 SSH MCP Tools v2 Features Guide

items.title: Context Object Overview
items.text:
The Context object provides access to MCP server capabilities and state:

from fastmcp import Context

async def my_tool(param: str, ctx: Context | None = None) -> str:
    if ctx:
        # Access logging
        ctx.logger.info("Tool started")

        # Report progress
        ctx.progress.update(25, "Processing...")

        # Access server state
        server_info = ctx.server.get_info()

        # Set custom metadata
        ctx.set_metadata("operation_id", "12345")

    return "Result"

        "user": connection.username
    })

        await asyncio.sleep(0.1)

items.title: Context Integration Examples
path: 🌟 SSH MCP Tools v2 Features Guide

items.title: Execute Command with Context
items.text:
@mcp.tool("execute-command")
async def execute_command(
    cmdString: str,
    connectionName: str | None = None,
    ctx: Context | None = None,
) -> str:
    ssh_manager = get_ssh_manager()

    # Log operation start
    if ctx:
        ctx.logger.info("Starting SSH command execution", {
            "command": cmdString,
            "connection": connectionName or "default",
            "command_length": len(cmdString)
        })

    try:
        # Report progress
        if ctx:
            ctx.progress.start("Executing command...")

        result = await ssh_manager.execute_command(cmdString, connectionName)

        # Report completion
        if ctx:
            ctx.progress.complete(f"Command completed ({len(result)} chars output)")
            ctx.logger.info("Command execution successful", {
                "output_length": len(result),
                "exit_status": "success"
            })

        return result.strip() if result else ""

    except Exception as error:
        if ctx:
            ctx.logger.error("Command execution failed", {
                "error": str(error),
                "error_type": type(error).__name__
            })
            ctx.progress.error(f"Failed: {str(error)}")

        return f"Error: {str(error)}"
path: 🌟 SSH MCP Tools v2 Features Guide

items.title: 📊 Enhanced Logging and Monitoring
path: 🌟 SSH MCP Tools v2 Features Guide

v2 uses Loguru for enhanced logging capabilities:

    "local_path": "/path/to/file.txt",

ctx.logger.info("File transfer progress", category="transfer")
ctx.logger.info("Command execution", category="command")

items.title: Metadata Benefits

  2. API Documentation: Automatic generation of API documentation

  4. Version Management: Track tool versions and compatibility

items.title: ⚡ Automatic Tool Registration
path: 🌟 SSH MCP Tools v2 Features Guide

items.title: Zero-Configuration Setup
items.text:
v1 Manual Registration: 

# Multiple manual registration calls required
def _register_tools(self) -> None:
    from .tools import (
        register_download_tool,
        register_execute_command_tool,
        register_list_servers_tool,
        register_upload_tool,
    )

    register_execute_command_tool(self.mcp, self._ssh_manager)
    register_upload_tool(self.mcp, self._ssh_manager)
    register_download_tool(self.mcp, self._ssh_manager)
    register_list_servers_tool(self.mcp, self._ssh_manager)

v2 Automatic Registration: 

# Tools automatically register when imported
from .ssh_tools import mcp  # All 4 tools now registered!

# No manual registration needed
path: 🌟 SSH MCP Tools v2 Features Guide

items.title: Registration Process
items.text:
1. Import Time: Tools register themselves when the module is imported
  2. Decorator Magic: @mcp.tool() decorator handles registration
  3. Global Registry: Tools are added to a global MCP instance
  4. Validation: Automatic validation of tool signatures and metadata
path: 🌟 SSH MCP Tools v2 Features Guide

items.title: Benefits
items.text:
• Reduced Boilerplate: No manual registration code
  • Error Prevention: Can't forget to register a tool
  • Consistency: All tools follow the same registration pattern
  • Maintainability: Easy to add new tools
path: 🌟 SSH MCP Tools v2 Features Guide

items.title: 📈 Progress Reporting
path: 🌟 SSH MCP Tools v2 Features Guide

items.title: Real-Time Progress Updates
items.text:
@mcp.tool("upload")
async def upload(
    localPath: str,
    remotePath: str,
    connectionName: str | None = None,
    ctx: Context | None = None,
) -> str:
    if ctx:
        ctx.progress.start("Preparing file upload...")

    try:
        # Get file info
        file_size = os.path.getsize(localPath)

        if ctx:
            ctx.progress.update(10, f"Uploading {os.path.basename(localPath)} ({file_size} bytes)")

        # Simulate upload progress
        for percent in range(20, 100, 10):
            if ctx:
                ctx.progress.update(percent, f"Uploading... {percent}%")
            await asyncio.sleep(0.1)  # Simulate work

        # Complete upload
        result = await ssh_manager.upload_file(localPath, remotePath, connectionName)

        if ctx:
            ctx.progress.complete("Upload completed successfully")

        return result

    except Exception as error:
        if ctx:
            ctx.progress.error(f"Upload failed: {str(error)}")
        raise
path: 🌟 SSH MCP Tools v2 Features Guide

items.title: Progress States
items.text:
• start(message): Begin operation with initial message
  • update(percent, message): Update progress with percentage and message
  • complete(message): Mark operation as completed
  • error(message): Mark operation as failed
  • warning(message): Add warning without changing state
path: 🌟 SSH MCP Tools v2 Features Guide

items.title: 🛡️ Error Handling Enhancements
path: 🌟 SSH MCP Tools v2 Features Guide

items.title: Structured Error Information
items.text:
try:
    result = await ssh_manager.execute_command(cmdString, connectionName)
    return result
except SSHConnectionError as error:
    if ctx:
        ctx.logger.error("SSH connection failed", {
            "error_type": "SSHConnectionError",
            "host": error.host,
            "port": error.port,
            "reason": str(error)
        })
    return f"Connection Error: {str(error)}"
except SSHAuthenticationError as error:
    if ctx:
        ctx.logger.error("SSH authentication failed", {
            "error_type": "SSHAuthenticationError",
            "username": error.username,
            "auth_method": error.auth_method
        })
    return f"Authentication Error: {str(error)}"
except Exception as error:
    if ctx:
        ctx.logger.error("Unexpected error", {
            "error_type": type(error).__name__,
            "error_message": str(error),
            "traceback": traceback.format_exc()
        })
    return f"Error: {str(error)}"
path: 🌟 SSH MCP Tools v2 Features Guide

items.title: Error Recovery and Retry
items.text:
@mcp.tool("execute-command")
async def execute_command_with_retry(
    cmdString: str,
    connectionName: str | None = None,
    ctx: Context | None = None,
    max_retries: int = 3
) -> str:
    for attempt in range(max_retries):
        try:
            if ctx and attempt > 0:
                ctx.logger.warning(f"Retry attempt {attempt}/{max_retries}")

            result = await ssh_manager.execute_command(cmdString, connectionName)
            return result

        except SSHConnectionError as error:
            if attempt < max_retries - 1:
                if ctx:
                    ctx.logger.warning(f"Connection failed, retrying... ({attempt + 1}/{max_retries})")
                await asyncio.sleep(2 ** attempt)  # Exponential backoff
                continue
            else:
                if ctx:
                    ctx.logger.error("All retry attempts failed")
                raise
